corpus.title: Binary candidate filter
corpus.text: Binary signatures scan compact Hamming codes first, over-fetch a candidate set, and then rerank selected documents with exact float-vector similarity.

corpus.title: Hamming hot path
corpus.text: Packed binary signatures use word-wise xor plus popcount. Hardware popcount, lookup fallbacks, and deterministic top-k selection keep the flat Hamming scan reproducible.

corpus.title: Multi-probe binary index
corpus.text: A production binary backend should avoid full scans by probing nearby buckets, MIH partitions, HNSW-Hamming, or another sublinear candidate-generation structure.

corpus.title: PCA projection
corpus.text: PCA learns a global real-valued projection from document vectors. It can be useful before binarization, but training and evaluation records must be separated.

corpus.title: ITQ rotation
corpus.text: ITQ rotates a PCA subspace with an orthogonal matrix to reduce quantization error before taking the sign of each projected coordinate.

corpus.title: Learned projection baseline
corpus.text: A global learned projection can train on pair differences or other document-only statistics. It must not leak evaluation queries into the encoder artifact.

corpus.title: Precomputed embedding artifact provenance
corpus.text: Frozen embedding artifacts record model, tokenizer, generator, dataset, qrels, prompt identities, normalization, dtype, and hash scopes.

corpus.title: Canonical artifact hash
corpus.text: Canonical artifact hashes should depend on parsed float32 payload bytes and deterministic metadata, not incidental JSON number spelling.

corpus.title: Generator requirements manifest
corpus.text: A fixture generator should parse the same requirements manifest whose SHA-256 appears in provenance, so the recorded environment matches runtime checks.

corpus.title: Affective memory security
corpus.text: Affective memory stores sensitive emotion-related signals and therefore needs consent policy, sensitivity labels, encryption, and audit-friendly provenance.

corpus.title: Encrypted local storage
corpus.text: Local memory files can use AEAD encryption with a strong key derivation function and atomic replace semantics to protect data at rest.

corpus.title: AEAD and KDF contract
corpus.text: AES-GCM or another AEAD mode protects confidentiality and integrity, while scrypt or another KDF turns a password or machine secret into an encryption key.

corpus.title: Urgency based memory routing
corpus.text: Urgency-aware retrieval gates short, medium, long, and base memory tiers so live chat does not wait for unnecessary deep retrieval.

corpus.title: Memory aware prompt builder
corpus.text: A prompt builder can inspect trigger keywords, urgency, and dialogue context to choose which memory layers should be queried before constructing the final prompt.

corpus.title: Memory tiers
corpus.text: Short, medium, long, and base memory tiers trade off latency, recency, durability, cross-agent reuse, and prompt budget.

corpus.title: MDBX storage primitives
corpus.text: MDBX-backed adapters persist documents and resource manifests while keeping storage, indexing, retrieval, and ingestion boundaries separate.

corpus.title: BM25 lexical retrieval
corpus.text: BM25 and BM25F lexical indexes use term frequency, inverse document frequency, and length normalization to rank sparse text matches.

corpus.title: Hybrid retrieval
corpus.text: Hybrid retrieval can combine lexical matches, dense vector similarity, binary candidate generation, and exact reranking.

corpus.title: Knowledge base capability
corpus.text: A knowledge-base capability retrieves trusted facts, project notes, and durable reference material without turning the memory library into a general agent framework.

corpus.title: Wiki style long-term synthesis
corpus.text: Long-term synthesis can consolidate repeated memories into wiki-like articles that are portable across agents and easier to audit.

corpus.title: Cross-agent memory
corpus.text: Cross-agent memory needs explicit source provenance, ownership, schema compatibility, and policies for sharing durable knowledge between agents.

corpus.title: Chunker layout
corpus.text: Chunkers split resources into stable retrievable units while preserving resource identifiers, revisions, and text offsets for targeted reindexing.

corpus.title: Resource indexer
corpus.text: A resource indexer coordinates document snapshots, embeddings, lexical postings, and vector indexes without collapsing those concerns into one storage facade.

corpus.title: Header include hygiene
corpus.text: Public headers should compile independently, while aggregate domain headers remain the preferred user-facing include surface.

corpus.title: Graded qrels
corpus.text: Graded relevance judgments distinguish highly relevant, partially relevant, and weakly relevant documents so nDCG can evaluate ranking quality.

corpus.title: No-answer and ignored query coverage
corpus.text: Evaluation reports must track judged, no-answer, and ignored query coverage by query identity, not just by count.

corpus.title: Experiment notes
corpus.text: Experiment notes record date, hypothesis, expected result, actual benchmark table, caveats, and follow-up checks for each research line.

corpus.title: Appraisal model
corpus.text: Affective memory can represent appraisal dimensions such as novelty, pleasantness, goal relevance, coping potential, and social meaning.

corpus.title: Affective tags
corpus.text: Emotion tags should be explicit derived projections with confidence, source, consent, and sensitivity metadata rather than hidden prompt state.

corpus.title: Consent policy
corpus.text: Storing personal or emotional memories requires policy hooks for consent, retention, erasure, visibility, and redaction.

corpus.title: HNSW Hamming backend
corpus.text: HNSW over binary codes can provide approximate nearest-neighbor candidate generation with Hamming distance instead of scanning every signature.

corpus.title: IVF and PQ hybrid
corpus.text: IVF and product quantization partition vector space and compress payloads so candidate generation can scale beyond flat scans.

corpus.title: Multi-index hashing
corpus.text: Multi-index hashing splits binary codes into substrings, probes matching buckets, and joins partial matches to recover nearby Hamming neighbors.

corpus.title: MiniLM embedding fixture
corpus.text: The MiniLM fixture uses a pinned transformer revision, mean pooling, L2 normalization, explicit float32 narrowing, and frozen JSON vectors.

corpus.title: BGE and E5 embeddings
corpus.text: Additional embedding adapters can support BGE, E5, or other popular models through precomputed fixtures before adding production runtime dependencies.

corpus.title: Precomputed fixture workflow
corpus.text: Precomputed embedding fixtures decouple benchmark verification from network access, model downloads, and provider-specific embedding runtime code.
